title: eupatorium daleoides
intro: Eupatorium daleoides, also known as Dale's Boneset, is a perennial plant native to the southeastern United States, belonging to the Asteraceae family. Traditionally, it has been used for its medicinal properties, including anti-inflammatory and antiseptic qualities, making it useful in treating fever, rheumatism, and skin conditions. The plant's health benefits include reducing pain and inflammation, as well as supporting immune function and wound healing. Herbal preparations of Eupatorium daleoides can be found in teas, tinctures, and infusions, and it is often combined with other herbs in traditional medicine to enhance its effects.
